sb_prompt: DISCUSS HOW YOU AND THE OTHER CALLER KEEP UP ON CURRENT EVENTS.      DO YOU GET MOST OF YOUR NEWS FROM TVOR PEOPLE YOU KNOW?  ARE YOU SATISFIED WITH THE QUALITY OF COVERAGE?
raw_output:
ParticipantA: Hey there! How are you doing today?
ParticipantB: I'm doing well, thanks. How about you?
ParticipantA: I'm doing okay. Just trying to keep up with the news these days. How about you?
ParticipantB: Same here. I try to stay informed about what's going on in the world.
ParticipantA: Yeah, it can be tough to keep up sometimes. Do you watch the news on TV or do you get it from other sources?
ParticipantB: I usually watch the news on TV, but I also read articles online and follow some news outlets on social media.
ParticipantA: That's interesting. I don't really watch TV that much, but I do read the newspaper sometimes.
ParticipantB: What do you think about the quality of news coverage these days? Do you think it's accurate and unbiased?
ParticipantA: Honestly, I don't really pay much attention to that. I just try to get the basic facts and move on.
ParticipantB: Yeah, I think it's important to be aware of any potential biases or inaccuracies in the news. That's why I try to get my news from a variety of sources and try to stay critical of what I'm reading.
ParticipantA: I can see how that would be helpful. I just kind of take things at face value most of the time.
ParticipantB: Well, it's important to be informed and to have a good understanding of what's going on in the world, but it's also important to approach the news with a critical eye and not to blindly accept everything we're told.
ParticipantA: Yeah, I agree. It's good to have a balanced perspective and to question what we're being told.
ParticipantB: Definitely. Well, it was nice talking to you.
ParticipantA: Same here. Have a good day.
